commit.message:
feat: Private AI Server — wizard setup, dynamic VPS per workspace

- Add /dashboard/private-server page with 3-step wizard
  - Choose: managed (AI DeskFlow servers) or custom (user's own VPS)
  - Auto-install via setup.sh through FastAPI orchestrator
  - Progress tracking with polling + animated UI
  - Security info panel (AES-256, local AI, encrypted transit)
- Add /api/vps routes (GET status, POST setup, DELETE remove)
- Add /api/vps/status for live health checks + install progress
- Modify fastapi.ts: resolveBackendUrl() supports per-workspace VPS
- Modify chat route: queries user's private VPS when configured
- Modify WhatsApp proxy: routes to user's VPS endpoint
- Add "Private Server" nav item with Shield icon in sidebar
- SSH credentials encrypted with AES-256-GCM, deleted after install

Fix LLM timeouts, restore tracer context detail, fix Ollama health check

- Increase HTTP_TIMEOUT from 60s to 120s for sync LLM calls
- Add streaming-specific timeout (300s total, 120s between chunks)
  to prevent premature timeout on long streaming responses
- Catch asyncio.TimeoutError explicitly in both sync and streaming
  paths with descriptive error messages
- Add include_context parameter to debug traces WebSocket command
  so frontend can request full context_messages in trace detail
- Fix Ollama health check stripping /v1 suffix before calling
  native endpoints (/api/tags)
- Rebuild frontend panel

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: align in-editor and dashboard export dimensions

Two root causes of the mismatch:

1. autoComputedHeight measured chartAreaRef (just the SVG area) instead of
   chartRef (full #chart-container including header/question/footer).
   Dashboard export got a smaller height than in-editor export.

2. getContainerDimensions() used offsetWidth/offsetHeight which includes the
   1px CSS border (+2px), but renderChartOffscreen has no border.
   Changed to clientWidth/clientHeight (border-free) so both in-editor and
   dashboard export use identical dimensions.

commit.message:
fix: proxy API calls through parent portal to avoid CORS

The NS API at edge.phoneware.cloud doesn't include GitHub Pages in its
CORS allowed origins. Instead of making direct fetch calls from the SPA,
route API requests through the parent portal via postMessage. The inject
script (running on the portal's allowed origin) makes the actual fetch
calls and sends results back to the SPA iframe.

commit.message:
fix: apply Codex 5.3 + GPT-5.4 audit findings (9 issues)

Codex 5.3 (6.3/10):
C-1 HIGH: android:allowBackup=false + explicit exclusion rules in
    backup_rules.xml / data_extraction_rules.xml — health data and
    encrypted prefs were previously exposed to cloud/device backup.
C-2 HIGH: ServerApiClient now takes baseUrl param (runtime QR URL) and
    derives cert-pin hostname from it — was always hitting SERVER_URL_DEFAULT.
    All 3 ViewModel callers updated to pass Config.getServerUrl().
C-3 HIGH: SyncQueueEntity unique index changed from (dataType,measuredAt)
    to SHA-256 recordHash of '$dataType|$payload' — prevents silent data
    loss when two devices record at the same timestamp. DB is ephemeral
    (fallbackToDestructiveMigration), no migration needed.
C-4 MEDIUM: ApiService distinguishes 4xx (PermanentSyncFailure) vs 5xx;
    SyncWorker maps permanent→Result.failure, transient→Result.retry.
    Stops infinite WorkManager retry on 401/403/bad-payload.
C-5 MEDIUM: Token fallback catches broadly — noted; narrowing to
    HealthConnectException requires SDK-specific error codes, deferred.
C-6 MEDIUM: HRV dedup uses ChronoUnit.MINUTES < 240 instead of HOURS < 4
    — fixes truncation bug introduced in previous commit (4h59m → 4 not 5).
C-7 MEDIUM: SettingsViewModel.pingClient built lazily with runtime hostname
    so cert pinning matches the QR-configured server, not always the default.

GPT-5.4 findings (unique, not covered by Codex):
G-1 HIGH: MainActivity.onCreate adds FLAG_SECURE — prevents health data
    appearing in Recents thumbnails and screenshots.
G-2 HIGH: BP anomaly and weekly summary notifications use
    VISIBILITY_PRIVATE + setPublicVersion (generic text on lock screen) —
    raw BP values and sleep metrics were previously visible without unlock.

DAO test updated: entity() helper with computeRecordHash(); added new test
'distinct payloads at same timestamp both inserted' to cover C-3 fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enforce methodology: split master corpus into active/archive datasets

Introduces a build-time data pipeline (`scripts/split.mjs`) that splits
the canonical master corpus into methodology-eligible (active) and
archived datasets. The split runs automatically before every build via
the `prebuild` npm script.

Methodology rules (all must be true for active):
  - vintageDate within rolling 5-year window (computed at build time)
  - fundNumber is I, II, or III
  - fundSizeM < 200

Dataset structure:
  - funds.master.json: full canonical corpus (949 funds)
  - funds.json: active / methodology-eligible (233 funds)
  - funds.archive.json: excluded with reasons (716 funds)

Archive reason breakdown:
  - outside_rolling_window: 659
  - fund_number_out_of_scope: 134
  - size_cap_exceeded: 42

Changes:
  - Renamed funds.json → funds.master.json (canonical corpus)
  - Added scripts/split.mjs with rolling cutoff logic
  - Added prebuild + data:split npm scripts
  - Updated cli/validate.ts to check all three datasets + integrity
  - Updated cli/import.ts to write to master
  - Updated methodology page with rolling window language
  - Updated README with dataset structure documentation
  - Static page bundle dropped from 77.5 kB to 32.3 kB (performance win)

All datasets pass Zod schema validation. Build succeeds.
active + archive == master integrity check passes.

Phase 10 + Evolution Batch 2: New parent strategies, regime equity analysis, targeted mutations

Phase 10 — New Parent Discovery:
- Donchian Trend (MNQ): PF=1.29, GRINDING filter → PF=1.76, Sharpe=3.38
- Keltner Channel (MNQ): PF=1.46, 340 trades — confirmed 5m exit degeneration
- VWAP Trend Continuation (MNQ-Long): PF=1.67, Sharpe=2.62 — best risk-adjusted
- 4-strategy portfolio: $11,113 PnL, Sharpe 3.15, Calmar 7.52, all correlations near zero
- Regime equity curves: Donchian bleeds in all 7 negative cells, GRINDING filter fixes it
- Key finding: HIGH_VOL_TRENDING_LOW_RV bleed is structural, not entry-model fixable

Evolution Batch 2 — Regime-gap-targeted mutations:
- New mutations: compute_ema_alignment, compute_range_fade
- 10 recipes targeting HIGH_VOL_TRENDING_LOW_RV and RANGING gaps
- Portfolio fitness scoring, regime coverage analysis
- Scheduler --queue flag for batch selection

commit.message:
fix: UFAS dump timeout 120s→600s, stdin DEVNULL, 진행 상황 로그 추가

- TIMEOUT: 120 → 600초 (10분) — 펌웨어 덤프 실제 소요 시간 반영
- stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL: sudo 패스워드 프롬프트 대기로 인한 hang 방지
- communicate() 블로킹 대신 background thread + 30초 poll 방식으로 교체:
    · 30초마다 "[UFAS] 진행 중... Ns 경과, 덤프 파일 크기: N bytes" 출력
    · 파일이 생성된 이후 크기 변화로 실제 진행 확인 가능
    · timeout 초과 시 SIGKILL 후 5초 추가 대기

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(sanely-jsoniter): port from jsoniter (MIT), Scala.js day one

Restructure ANALYSIS.md with a clear three-strategy architecture:

- PORT (~70%): proven parsing code from jsoniter-scala (MIT licensed)
  — Eisel-Lemire float/double, UTF-8, string escaping, integers
- KEEP (macro): existing codec-layer wins that already beat jsoniter
  — direct constructors, typed locals, branchless encoding, @switch
- REWRITE (~16%): new optimizations unlocked by owning macro + parser
  — field-order prediction, compile-time key bytes, fused parsing,
    eliminated virtual dispatch, pre-sized write buffers

Adds Scala.js day-one support plan (jsoniter's JVM/JS are 99% identical),
unified component map showing each piece's strategy + impact, and
projected combined performance: +15-36% reads, +32-40% writes vs
jsoniter-scala native.

feat: v1.1.35 — per-agent subdir isolation for isolate: false

When isolate: false, multiple agents share the same cwd. Previously
settings.local.json (Stop hook config) written to shared .claude/ could
collide between concurrent agents. Now each agent uses .agent/{agent_id}/
as its Claude Code project directory, eliminating all file collisions.

- ClaudeCodeAgent._agent_work_dir(): returns .agent/{id}/ when not isolated
- All per-agent files (context, api key, CLAUDE.md, commands, Stop hook)
  written to agent subdir; shared context_files still copied to shared cwd
- Claude launched from subdir (cd .agent/{id} && claude)
- pre_trust_worktree called with subdir for correct trust registration
- 13 new tests in test_agent_subdir_isolation.py (2671 → 2684 total)
- Demo: 20/20 checks PASSED (2 parallel isolate:false agents, no collision)
